criteria_results.criterion: ExportPdfOptions interface has an optional stepTools field typed as an array of { step_id: string; tool: { cost_per_month: number | null } }
criteria_results.evidence: StepToolForExport interface at pdf.ts:20, optional stepTools field at pdf.ts:32 matching specified type signature

criteria_results.criterion: computeStepMonthlyCost() sums tool cost_per_month values for the step in addition to labor cost
criteria_results.evidence: computeStepMonthlyCost() at pdf.ts:725 accepts toolsMap param; buildStepToolsMap() at pdf.ts:712 groups tools by step_id; tool costs summed alongside labor

criteria_results.criterion: canvas-view.tsx handleEnhancedExportPdf fetches stepTools (via fetchStepToolsByStep or batch) and passes them to createWorkspacePdf when config.costAnalysis is enabled
criteria_results.evidence: canvas-view.tsx line 15 imports fetchStepToolsByStep; lines 311-351 use Promise.allSettled batch fetch gated on costAnalysis; exportStepTools passed to createWorkspacePdf at line 351

criteria_results.criterion: Steps with zero labor cost but non-zero tool cost still appear in cost table
criteria_results.evidence: pdf.ts line 517 changed filter from .filter(s => s.hours > 0) to .filter(s => s.hours > 0 || s.cost > 0); same fix at line 603

criteria_results.criterion: TypeScript compiles with zero errors (npx tsc --noEmit)
criteria_results.evidence: BUILD_RESULT_1.json: typecheck status=pass, errors=0, output='npx tsc --noEmit — no output (clean)'

criteria_results.criterion: When tools array is empty, a centered empty state message is visible on the tools canvas
criteria_results.evidence: tools-canvas-view.tsx line 479: condition {tools.length === 0 && toolSections.length === 0} renders empty state with heading 'No tools yet'. Test workspace has 3 existing tool section nodes so empty state correctly hidden — this is expected behavior, not a failure.

criteria_results.criterion: Message text guides user to add their first tool
criteria_results.evidence: Per BUILD_RESULT_2.json: existing implementation at lines 479-506 includes centered heading 'No tools yet' with body text guiding user and inline Add buttons. Pre-existing implementation confirmed correct by builder.

criteria_results.criterion: Styling follows dark theme conventions (text-white/55 or text-white/30 for secondary text)
criteria_results.evidence: BUILD_RESULT_2.json notes: uses var(--text-secondary)/var(--text-tertiary) — dark theme equivalents of text-white/55 and text-white/30

criteria_results.criterion: Disabled checkboxes in export dialog show a tooltip on hover explaining the prerequisite
criteria_results.evidence: Export PDF dialog opened. Evaluated all labels in dialog: 0 labels have a title attribute, 0 checkboxes are disabled. All SECTION_GROUPS entries have available: true hardcoded — no section is ever disabled, making tooltip infrastructure unreachable. title={!available ? disabledTooltip : undefined} correctly renders on disabled labels, but no labels are ever disabled.

criteria_results.criterion: Tooltip text is contextual per section (e.g., 'Add a journey tab to enable' for journey sections)
criteria_results.evidence: Tooltip strings are correctly defined in SECTION_GROUPS (e.g., journeyMap: 'Add a journey tab to enable', improvements: 'Requires improvement ideas'), but are never rendered at runtime because available is always true. Cannot be verified in browser.

criteria_results.criterion: Tooltips only appear on disabled sections, not enabled ones
criteria_results.evidence: title attribute is conditioned on !available — correct logic. Since available is always true, this criterion technically holds (no enabled sections show tooltips, no disabled sections exist to test).

bugs_found.description: IMP-094 tooltip implementation is structurally present but functionally unreachable. All SECTION_GROUPS entries have available: true hardcoded as a static constant. The title attribute tooltip is correctly conditioned on !available, but since no section is ever available=false, no tooltip ever appears. The 'disabled' state for sections (opacity-50, cursor-not-allowed, checkbox disabled) is also never triggered.
bugs_found.steps_to_reproduce: 1. Open any workspace canvas. 2. Click Export PDF. 3. Observe that all checkboxes are enabled and none are grayed out. 4. Inspect labels in DevTools — none have a title attribute.

improvements_found.observation: The export dialog's section availability should be computed dynamically from workspace data (e.g., journeyMap disabled when workspace has no journey tab, improvements disabled when improvementIdeas array is empty). Currently all sections are statically available=true. This would make the tooltip feature actually functional and improve the UX by clarifying why sections might not produce output.
improvements_found.design_principle: Nielsen H1: Visibility of system status — users should understand when a section requires prerequisites
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Accept availability flags as props (hasJourneyTab, hasImprovements, hasAiInsights, hasPerspectives, hasPrioritizationScores, hasTools) and compute available per section based on these flags. Pass them down from canvas-view.tsx which already has access to tabs, improvementIdeas, etc.
